item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image is owned by Richard Vantielcke, not under an explicit Creative Commons license. As such, it is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0 and has since been removed.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edits
- Hide

To link to similar awcy content, I propose the following crosslink:

Infected individuals will ignore any written information not contained within a printed box, such as the ones on this document, and if confronted about this will become irritable and often violent.

to

Infected individuals will ignore any written information not contained within a printed box, such as the ones on this document, and if confronted about this will become irritable and often violent.1

Footnotes
1. This behavior is similar to the reaction of individuals exposed to SCP-2274 when attempting to remove said anomaly from the vicinity of the victim. Investigation into a link between these two cognitohazards is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Likes the concept, don't like the "D for disposables" institutional mindset. This time it's a memetic effect, but what would the Foundation do if it's something like, say, the Murder Knife.

It's both cynical AND shortsighted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like AWCY? as petulant hipsters… sometimes. This is one of those times. This is really well done, I think. The format screw seems minor by today's standards, and at least it makes narrative sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AWCY YOU DIIIIICKS!!!
*upvoted*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ugh that image is so good but I can't upvote this because it's a Dumb Pun Article (tm)
Even though it makes sense as an in character conceit
I like it but I hate it
It's cool, it's not cool
Ughhhhhhhhh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably my favorite AWCY scp yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Found this thanks to a typo on another SCP. Thank you typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit, that is the creepiest, fucking, cardboard box ever! And the usage of AWCY? is spot on. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AWCY? is a bunch of fucking prats and I hope they get run over by an armored car that's on fire.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Elaborate, please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nnnnnope. -2

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That goes back to the whole Octopi vs. Octopuses debate (which, might I add, Octopuses is now accepted as correct.) But I defer to your findings, good sir. Just sounds awkward to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah it's a legit thing, modi operandi is the plural form of the usual modus operandi. Just means they have multiple methods of operation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Correct me if I'm wrong, but I believe you meant "Modus Operandi", not "Modi." Modus Operandi is the way you generally do things, which in itself is plural. Modi Operandi is like saying "sheeps" or "deers."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it's intentional. The idea is that the infected (who are usually working with the object, and would be looking at this document) would only read something within a printed box. Hence the format. The second paragraph is about the messages the infected can't see, and they become hostile if anybody brings up the messages. But the messages have to be documented, so the researchers came up with a meta solution to the problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sites not normally dealing with memetic hazards may not be sufficiently prepared to counteract an infection of this subtlety. Since it doesn't start out particularly hostile, it could be overlooked by the more inexperienced poor schmucks who are first tasked with investigating it, and then snowball from there without much resistance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me or is anybody else bothered at how a Site would have such terrible security against memetic threats?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for that. Now take your +1 and be happy. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing that the format is intentional. Or at least most of it - is the second paragraph of the Description supposed to be normal, or should that be in a box as well?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's very well-written, and I really like the format screw.

…but it's so on-the-nose and eye rolling and not in a way that's remotely charming. :/

I feel like this could have been great with more creative effort, even though that's a bummer to say since there clearly was already a pretty significant amount of effort that went into this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
why this is just the sort of self congratulatory back patting pablum that makes me completely and utterly loathe AWCY

many people take this to be a sign of success but they are objectively wrong about everything, as proved by this

memetic image
!!A SKELETON JUMPED OUT!!
!!A SKELETON JUMPED OUT!!

item.title: Re: EXPLANATIONS
item.content:encoded: Wow fuck, that last line just flipped my vote. wth

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops. Meant to fix that but forgot in all the excitement, sorry about that. Fix'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really just don't like this at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very clever! This fits in very well with my image of AWCY. the format screw is amusing and pretty clever. I enjoy the various meta levels of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've never wanted to punch a fictional organization in the face so much.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just goes to show that even AWCY? doesn't always design something solely to mass-kill people for an odd, eccentric reason. Sometimes they do things just to tick people off.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one.

Of course, I also happen to doodle little squares and cubes and find those shapes pleasing and oddly comforting. Right now, about a foot to my left, is a notepad I scribble on during meetings. At the bottom of the page are four neat rows of thirty or so little squares each. Don't even get me started on how I've played Minecraft.

Personal eccentricities aside, I like the use of formatting in this SCP and the story it tells. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey! Fix your image! i can't see the Sandbox from this location, and the fact that the image is broken is, I think, actively ruining the article for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually don't mind this format screw. I wonder why. Upvotes for you good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally, awcy worthy of the name. p. cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just when I think we've run out of good "format screw" ideas, this comes along… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Art must allow a viewer to find their way to the meaning of the piece, however the exhibition is meant to prompt that. The people exposed to the item aren't aware of its effects at all, but me and you sure are, and so would any Foundation researchers assigned to it. The absence of shark isn't going to mean much to people getting mauled by it, but someone finding the note would make the connection. The people being killed or mind-wiped are, by definition, not the target audience of the piece.

And leaving art out of it in order to more directly make their point is out of the question. That's the exact opposite of art — it's the kind of indoctrination that art is against. From their perspective, they're taking the high road here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THIS is AWCY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like everyone else, i saw the page and just thought "look at this dumb idiot and his dumb idiot formatting" but then i kept reading and i must say that i really like what you've done with this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was worried that this would be another "look at the format screw, well guess what the SCP did that THE END" article, but lo and behold, actual content! And a point, to boot!

Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's actually brilliant

Edit: this was meant for the above explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's clever, but I always hate it when AWCY teases the Foundation. Especially when they sound twelve doing so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This does nothing for me at all, and I like AWCY? and I really like it when people tease the Foundation. But this is just dull and uninteresting. Maybe seeing that it was AWCY? before reading it robbed it of its kick, but this came across as someone thinking that they were too clever for you sheeple rather than actually being clever.

The boxes around the containment procedure were kind of nice, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. I love this so much.

It is pleasing to my head-canon of a stagnant and foolish Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's clever, and I always enjoy it when AWCY teases the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said in the chat, I have the same problem with this as I have with many of the AWCY? scps: if you have the power to directly and forcibly alter someone's patterns of thought, why are you using it to directly alter perceptions of people, in order to suggest a message, that might cause other people to think about things and change their own perceptions to what you want to convey? Hell, the entire concept renders all art meaningless, but that's neither here nor there: these people want people to think a certain way. They are narcissistic psychopaths who place no value on the lives or identities of other human beings. They have the capacity to directly and forcibly alter someone's thoughts. Why are they being so circuitous about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A big thanks to everyone in chat who helped out!

The image came from http://www.ludimaginary.net

item.title: EXPLANATIONS
item.content:encoded:
<Voct> there's this weird-ass effect, and it makes no sense until you read the little explanatory text
<Voct> and then you suddenly perceive everything differently
<Voct> again, this is AWCY taunting the Foundation
<Voct> "Think outside the box. OH WAIT, YOU CAN'T"
<Voct> so many layers
<Voct> because what is the Foundation if not an organization that puts things in boxes
<Voct> not just physical boxes (containing them)
<Voct> but *conceptual* boxes (demystifying them)
<Voct> and the art literally goes *over the head* of those who are infected
<Voct> that's lovely
<Voct> you still have the original, yes?
<Voct> I suggest that instead of "Ideas don't have agendas"
<Voct> you put "ideas don't have ideology"
<TwistedGears> I like that
<Voct> 'Art can't be contained"
<Voct> in this case, the art is what is containING
<Voct> the art being the ultra-simplistic smily face drawn on the box
<Voct> and also, the art is what the afflicted person constructs about himself
<Voct> he builds his own containment
<Voct> it contains him
<Voct> and oh, the agoraphobia
<Voct> he does not feel secure and protected unless he is contained

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First upvote! :D I like this a lot. But then, I would. Nice work, TwistedGears!
